name: gpu_path_trace
title: GPU ray traced rendering
short_description: Path traced render of a bundled scene on the graphics processor.

structured_text.description: Renders a bundled scene with a path tracer that runs on the graphics processor, tracing rays and accumulating samples toward a clean image. The scene, sample budget and resolution are fixed, and the work uses the GPU's ray tracing and compute paths together.
structured_text.metric_explanation: The score is the average number of accumulated frames produced per second, reported in fps, which scales with how quickly the image converges.
structured_text.value_interpretation: Higher is better. A higher rate means faster previews and shorter final renders when working with ray traced lighting.
structured_text.reasoning: GPU path tracing is now central to high end content creation and to ray traced game effects, and it stresses both ray traversal and shading on the GPU.

domain_gaming.explanation: Ray traced lighting and reflections are an increasing part of modern games.

domain_office.explanation: Office machines do not run path tracing.

domain_software_development.explanation: Relevant mainly to developers building rendering tools.

domain_graphics_design.explanation: Product and concept visuals are increasingly rendered with GPU path tracing.

domain_video_editing.explanation: Rendered 3D inserts for video are produced with these renderers.

domain_3d_rendering.explanation: GPU path tracing is now a primary tool for offline 3D rendering.

domain_audio_production.explanation: Audio work has no rendering component.

domain_cad_engineering.explanation: Photoreal design review relies on GPU ray traced rendering.

domain_scientific_computing.explanation: High quality scientific visualisation uses ray traced rendering.

domain_ai_training.explanation: Photoreal synthetic data is generated with path traced renders.

domain_data_science.explanation: Analytics does not use path tracing.

domain_server_virt.explanation: GPU render farms run this workload across many nodes.
